characters.name: 1st Lieutenant Norman S. Dike Jr.
characters.description: 1st Lieutenant Norman S. Dike Jr.RankLieutenant ColonelBornMay 19th, 1918DiedJune 23rd, 1989NicknameFoxhole NormanActorPeter O'Meara 1st Lieutenant Norman S. Dike Jr.was a commander ofEasy Companyduring the Battle of the Bulge in Bastogne, Belgium. In the series he is portrayed as an unqualified field officer, resulting in several Easy Company men's deaths during their attack on Foy. He became known as "Foxhole Norman". Lieutenant Dike was born in Brooklyn, New York. He was a graduate of Brown University and was the son of a New York State Supreme Court judge. His mother was from the famous New York "Biddle jewelry family".

characters.name: 2nd Lieutenant James L. Diel
characters.description: 2nd Lieutenant James L. DielRank2nd LieutenantBorn3 March 1922Died19 September 1944NicknameLee 2nd Lieutenant James L. Dielwas a member ofEasy Companyof the101st Airborne Divisionduring World War Two. James L. Diel, was born on 3rd March 1922 in Jasper County, Illinois. He received his education at Newton High School, graduating in 1940. In the summers of 1939 and 1940, he took part in a military training which lasted 6 weeks and was held at the Jefferson Barracks school in St. Louis, Missouri.

characters.name: Alton More
characters.description: Alton MoreRankPrivateActorDoug Allen PrivateAlton M. Morewas a Soldier in Easy Company. Alton More was born in Casper, Wyoming on 23 April 1920. In 1942, he married a Wyoming girl. More was in the 115th U.S. Cavalry, Wyoming National Guard before joining the Paratroopers.

characters.name: Antonio C. Garcia
characters.description: Antonio C. GarciaRankTechnician 5th Grade Pvt.NicknameTony Techinician Fifth GradeAntonio C. “Tony” Garciawas a replacement forEasy Companyand was put intoSergeant Denver "Bull" Randleman's squad. He is played in the series by actor Douglas Spain. Born on October 15th, 1924, Garcia was a native of Inez, Texas, and was a high school student in Cheyenne, Wyoming when he enlisted as a Paratrooper. He borrowed a friend's prescription medicine to cover up a heart murmur for his Army physical and was accepted for training in 1943.

Speirswas the leader of Dog Company in the 2nd Battalion, 506th PIR of the 101st Airborne. His unit was absorbed into Easy Company during Bastogne and he was assigned as Company Commander after relieving1st Lieutenant Dikeof duty during the assault on Foye, Belgium.

characters.name: Captain Herbert Sobel
characters.description: Captain Herbert W. SobelRankCaptain (Retired as Lieutenant Colonel)BornJanuary 26th, 1912DiedSeptember 30th, 1987NicknameN/aActorDavid Schwimmer Captain Herbert W. Sobel, was the first commander and drill Instructor ofEasy Company. Herbert W. Sobel was born on 26 January 1912 in Chicago, Illinois to a Jewish family. He went to military school at Culver Military Academy in Indiana and graduated from the University of Illinois in 1933. Sobel is depicted as a very strict commander, who pushes the men to their limits in order to make his company the finest in the Battalion. Dick Winters attributes Sobel's overbearance and strictness with making Easy Company the success that it became. While Sobel was a "fantastic trainer", his tactical abilities in the field were poor; he was good at taking orders, but was poor at map-reading and had a tendency to panic if an unforeseen situation occurred. He was also easily agitated by his soldiers. George Luz in particularly pretending to be Major Horton convinced Sobel to cut through a farm's fence, leading to the release of several head of cattle, frustrating his superiors. He was also shown to be vindictive and feared the possibility of losing his leadership role in Easy Company as he attempted to sabotage Winters' rising popularity with the company by having him officially reprimanded on flimsy charges to get him out of the D-Day invasion. The result of his unsatisfactory combat and tactical ability caused the NCO's of Easy Company to lose confidence in Sobel's combat leadership, leading to his eventual transfer.

characters.name: Captain Lewis Nixon
characters.description: Captain Lewis NixonRankCaptainBornSeptember 30th, 1918DiedJanuary 11th, 1995NicknameNix, LewActorRon Livingston  Captain Lewis Nixon was the S-2 (Intelligence Officer) for the 2nd Battalion, 506th Parachute Infantry Regiment, 101st Airborne Division. Nixon was a friendly, easy-going officer described by Winters as "a free spirit who enjoyed the wild life and partied with the best of them." While Nixon was an exemplary officer in the field, he never fired his carbine in the line of duty, despite serving in Holland, Normandy and Bastogne; the latter particularly surprising according to Winters. Nixon was rather cynical of his lack of action, leading to a dismal outlook on the war after he lost his squad when jumping in to Germany. He wasn't away from the front lines however, almost being shot in the head when the Company retreated from their attack on a town in Holland, saved by his helmet and was an observer in reserve in other battles such as the battle of Foy.

He had 2 brothers, Michael John Mellett and John E. Mellett.

characters.name: T-4 Eugene Roe
characters.description: T-4 Eugene RoeRankTechnician 4th GradeBornOctober 17th, 1922DiedDecember 30th, 1998NicknameDocActorShane Taylor Eugene Gilbert "Doc" Roe Sr.was a medic and Paratrooper inEasy Company. He was the main focus of the episodeBastogne, where he befriended a Belgian nurse named Renée and discussed what treating the men felt like, as depicted in the miniseries,Band of Brothers. Eugene was born in Bayou Chene, Louisiana, on 17 October 1922 to Edward Malcomb and Maude Cecile Roe. He went by the nicknames "Gene" and "Bud". He had 2 sisters and 2 brothers; Minnie Eta Green (Roe), Winnie Elmira Smallman (Roe), Charles Horace Roe and John Everrett Roe. He quit school in his elementary school years.

characters.name: Technical Sergeant Donald Malarkey
characters.description: Technical Sergeant Donald MalarkeyRankTechnical SergeantBornJuly 31, 1921DiedSeptember 30, 2017NicknameMalark, DonActorScott Grimes T/Sgt. Donald George “Don” Malarkeywas an NCO from Easy Company, serving as a mortarman and later platoon sergeant in Second Platoon. Donald Malarkey was born in Astoria, Oregon on 31 July 1921, to Leo John “Tick” and Helen Adell Malarkey, and had 4 brothers named Leo, Robert, John and Bob, and a sister named Marilyn. He had two uncles that fought in World War I. One of them, Gerald, was killed by shrapnel in 1918, while the other, Robert, was gassed, and later died in 1926.

characters.name: George H. Smith Jr
characters.description: George H. Smith, Jr.RankPrivateActorIain Robertson George H. Smith, Jr.was a private in the506th Regimentof the101st Airborne. He participated in many battles, from the Battle of Normandy, through Operation Market Garden and the Battle of Bastogne and Foy, to Haguenau and into Germany. He had an incident with Talbert when he accidentally stabbed him with his bayonet mistaking Talbert for a kraut. This incident was known as “The Night of The Bayonet”. George Smith was in the same platoon asRichard Winters. He began training at Camp Toccoa, which included running up the Currahee Mountain. The training continued at Camp Mackall, from where he would head to Aldbourne in England for his next assignment.

characters.name: George Higgins
characters.description: George HigginsRankCorporalActorUnknown Corporal George Higginswas an enlisted man with the men inEasy Company. George Higgins was born in 1918. He enlisted into Easy Company and was trained at Camp Toccoa byCaptain Herbert Sobel. In Aldborne England, he was in the same squad as Sobel also was one to hear Technician 4th Grade George Luz mimic Major Oliver Horton. He once carried a Thompson Machine Gun.

characters.name: German Girl in Bed
characters.description: German Girl in BedRankCivilianBorn1924NicknamenoneActorIsabella Seibert The German Girl in Bed, as featured in the credits, is a minor character featured in theBand of Brothersin the episode, "Why We Fight". She lives on a rural German farm in Landsberg, along with her older sister. She slept with Janovec and had sex with him until Speirs arrives, interrupting them.

characters.name: German Girl with Eggs
characters.description: German Girl with EggsRankFarmerBorn1922NicknamenoneActorKristina Branden The German girl with the eggs, is a minor character inBand of Brothers.  She is featured in the episode "Why We Fight". She is the older sister of theGerman Girl in Bed, as they both live on the farm together. when the soldiers arrived, she collected eggs for them to eat for breakfast. She slapped one soldier for trying to groom her. She and her sister are alive.
